elements.sent-2411-001: The rapid advancement of diffusion models has greatly improved video synthesis, especially in controllable video generation, which is essential for applications like autonomous driving.
elements.sent-2411-002: However, existing methods are limited by scalability and how control conditions are integrated, failing to meet the needs for high-resolution and long videos for autonomous driving applications.
elements.sent-2411-003: In this paper, we introduce \methodname, a novel approach based on the DiT architecture, and tackle these challenges.
elements.sent-2411-004: Our method enhances scalability through flow matching and employs a progressive training strategy to manage complex scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-005: By incorporating spatial-temporal conditional encoding, \methodname achieves precise control over spatial-temporal latents.
elements.sent-2411-006: Comprehensive experiments show its superior performance in generating realistic street scene videos with higher resolution and more frames.
elements.sent-2411-007: With the rapid development of diffusion models, video synthesis~\cite{videoworldsimulators2024,ho2022video,blattmann2023stable,blattmann2023align has seen significant advancements.}
elements.sent-2411-008: Controllable video synthesis~\cite{li2024trackdiffusion,hu2024animate greatly facilitates the use of synthetic data in numerous downstream applications~\cite{wang2024detdiffusion .}}
elements.sent-2411-009: Especially in the field of autonomous driving, technologies like MagicDrive~\cite{magicdrive and DriveDreamer~\cite{wang2023drivedreamer exemplify how controllable multi-view video synthesis, capable of generating high-quality street-view videos, effectively aid downstream tasks, and enhance the performance and reliability of autonomous systems.}}
elements.sent-2411-010: High-resolution and long video synthesis is a focal point of research in the content generation domain~\cite{ma2024unleashing,chen2024pixart , and is highly demanded by autonomous driving technology.}
elements.sent-2411-011: Specifically, autonomous driving perception models require higher-resolution inputs to discern details and distant objects within the data~\cite{liang2022bevfusion .}
elements.sent-2411-012: The application of video synthesis in 3D reconstruction also necessitates high resolution to capture intricate details~\cite{gao2024magicdrive3d,zhao2024drivedreamer4d .}
elements.sent-2411-013: On the other hand, long videos provide more content for more comprehensively evaluating autonomous driving algorithms~\cite{yang2024drivearena .}
elements.sent-2411-014: From a technical perspective, high resolution is a straightforward method to enhance generation quality~\cite{chen2024pixart , while long videos encompass more dynamic content~\cite{xing2023dynamicrafter , aiding models in effectively learning the laws of the world~\cite{videoworldsimulators2024 .}}}

elements.sent-2411-015: However, synthesizing high-resolution long videos presents two major challenges.
elements.sent-2411-016: Firstly, the increase in data volume require the generative model's high scalability to support high-quality synthesis effectively.
elements.sent-2411-017: Secondly, geometry control in video generation poses significant challenges.
elements.sent-2411-018: Previous controllable techniques are primarily designed for image generation~\cite{magicdrive,wang2023drivedreamer,wen2024panacea,wang2024driving,ma2024unleashing,jiang2024dive , focusing on spatial latent spaces.}
elements.sent-2411-019: Achieving high-resolution long video synthesis necessitates a 3D compressed VAE that integrates spatial and temporal information~\cite{videoworldsimulators2024,opensora,yang2024cogvideox,pku_yuan_lab_and_tuzhan_ai_etc_2024_10948109 .}
elements.sent-2411-020: Utilizing spatial-temporal latents renders previous control methods inadequate, as demonstrated in Figure \ref{fig:control .}
elements.sent-2411-021: This necessitates novel approaches to effectively manage elements in synthetic videos, ensuring both spatial and temporal coherence with the given conditions.
elements.figure-2411-002: \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figs/control.pdf} \iftoggle{cvprfinal}{ }{ } \caption{Different from spatial latent~\cite{magicdrive,wen2024panacea,wang2024driving,ma2024unleashing,jiang2024dive}, spatial-temporal latent (ours) requires spatial-temporal condition injection for geometry controls (we omit the text condition here).} \label{fig:control} \iftoggle{cvprfinal}{ }{ }
elements.sent-2411-022: In this paper, we introduce \methodname, a novel approach based on the DiT architecture, designed to address the above challenges for high-resolution and long street-view video synthesis with precise control.
elements.sent-2411-023: We adopt the flow matching~\cite{esser2024scaling formulation for diffusion models, implementing it with a DiT-based architecture~\cite{peebles2023scalable,opensora , to enhance scalability.}}
elements.sent-2411-024: This improves the model's capacity to efficiently handle complex data across diverse scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-025: To enhance controllability, we use specially designed spatial-temporal conditional encoding for spatial-temporal latents from CogVAE~\cite{yang2024cogvideox , enabling precise management of representations.}
elements.sent-2411-026: Besides, we adopt a progressive bootstrapping strategy for model training, transitioning from short to long videos, allowing the model to capture intricate details and generalize to complex scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-027: Additionally, we utilize videos of various resolutions and durations to strengthen the model's generalization ability, enabling the model to synthesize longer videos than those for training.
elements.sent-2411-028: Our \methodname excels in generating highly realistic videos that align with road maps, 3D bounding boxes, and different camera perspectives, achieving higher resolution and more frames than previous works~\cite{magicdrive,gao2024vista,ma2024unleashing,jiang2024dive .}
elements.sent-2411-029: Comprehensive experiments and comparisons demonstrate the effectiveness of our training and control methods, significantly improving controllable street view video synthesis.
elements.sent-2411-030: In summary, the main contributions of this paper include:.
elements.sent-2411-031: We design an efficient framework, \methodname, leveraging progressive bootstrapping to achieve high-quality high-resolution long video generation.
elements.sent-2411-032: We develop novel spatiotemporal control for object positions, road semantics, and camera trajectories while maintaining multi-frame, multi-view consistency.
elements.sent-2411-033: Our model generalizes well from image to video generation through mixed-resolution and duration training, with extrapolation capabilities, significantly exceeding the resolution and frame number in previous works.

elements.sent-2411-049: Given a sequence of frame descriptions \(\{\mb{S _{t}\}, t \in \{0, \dots, T\}\), the goal is to generate corresponding street-view videos from latent variables \(\mb{z \sim \mathcal{N (\mb{0 , \boldsymbol{I )\), i.e., \(\{\mb{I _{c,t}\} = \mathcal{G (\{\mb{S _{t}\}, \mb{z )\), where \(c \in \{0, \dots, C\}\) denotes the \(C\) camera views.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-050: Here, high resolution indicates that \(\mb{I}\) has a high resolution, and long video implies that \(T\) is large.

elements.sent-2411-053: All geometric information, except for \(\mb{Tr _{t ^{0}\), is parameterized according to the local LiDAR coordinate of the ego car.}}
elements.sent-2411-055: For high-resolution image generation, \citet{rombach2021highresolution propose Latent Diffusion Models (LDMs), using a pre-trained VAE for image downsampling and diffusion models for latent generation.}
elements.sent-2411-056: This manner is widely adopted in both image generation~\cite{chenpixart,esser2024scaling and video generation~\cite{blattmann2023stable,opensora,videoworldsimulators2024 .}}
elements.sent-2411-057: With the latest advancement on diffusion models, \citet{esser2024scaling propose to train large scale diffusion models through simulation-free rectified flow~\cite{liuflow,lipman2023flow and v-prediction loss~\cite{esser2024scaling : \begin{equation}\mb{z}_{t} = (1 - t) x_{0} + t\epsilon L_{CFM} = E_{\epsilon\sim N(0,I)}\|v_{\Theta}(\mb{z}_{t},t)-\frac{1}{1-t}(\mb{z}-\epsilon)\|_{2}^{2},\end{equation} where $t\sim\operatorname{lognorm (0,1)$ is timestep and $v_{\Theta}$ is the model.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-058: For model architecture, depicted in \figref{fig:overview , \methodname introduces a novel DiT-based diffusion model for controllable street-view generation, utilizing STDiT-3 blocks as per \citet{opensora .}}
elements.sent-2411-059: These blocks feature separate spatial and temporal modules to enhance information processing.
elements.sent-2411-060: The architecture design incorporates two significant modifications.
elements.sent-2411-061: First, to facilitate multi-view generation, the Multi-View DiT (MVDiT) block integrates a cross-view attention layer~\cite{magicdrive , as shown on the left of \figref{fig:overview .}}
elements.sent-2411-062: Second, given the need for handling multiple control elements, \methodname employs cross-attention~\cite{rombach2021highresolution for text, boxes, camera views, and trajectories, alongside an addictive branch~\cite{zhang2023adding for maps to inject control signals.}}
elements.sent-2411-063: Nevertheless, the spatial encoding of control signals~\cite{magicdrive,ma2024unleashing,wen2024panacea,wang2024driving,zhao2024drivedreamer2 is incompatible with spatial-temporal latents, as illustrated in \figref{fig:control .}}
elements.sent-2411-064: Consequently, the encoder for each control signal is reformulated, exemplified by the spatial-temporal box encoder on the right of \figref{fig:overview .}
elements.sent-2411-066: Furthermore, \methodname employs a progressive bootstrap training strategy (\secref{sec:bootstrap ), transitioning from images to low-resolution and short videos, and ultimately to high-resolution and long videos, to enhance diffusion model convergence.}
elements.sent-2411-067: The final training stage incorporates variable length and resolution videos, allowing the model to generate images and videos at diverse resolutions and extrapolate to longer frame counts beyond the training settings.
elements.sent-2411-069: Training diffusion models for high-resolution and long video generation is computationally intensive and requires substantial GPU memory.
elements.sent-2411-070: DiT and 3D VAE are pivotal in scaling diffusion models in these aspects.
elements.sent-2411-071: As noted by \citet{peebles2023scalable , there is a strong negative correlation between model Gflops and FID, making DiT preferable to architectures like UNet~\cite{magicdrive for computational efficiency.}}
elements.sent-2411-072: For GPU memory, 3D VAE offers temporal downsampling.
elements.sent-2411-073: A typical 2D VAE~\cite{rombach2021highresolution compresses images by a factor of 8 in both height and width, achieving a $64\times$ compression ratio.}
elements.sent-2411-074: In contrast, 3D VAE~\cite{yang2024cogvideox compresses temporal information by $4\times$, resulting in a $256\times$ compression ratio\footnote{We ignore possible discrepancy in the dimension of latents through our discussion.}}
elements.sent-2411-075: Additionally, the trend of architectural unification~\cite{sun2024autoregressive allows DiT to utilize advanced parallelization methods, \eg, sequence parallelism~\cite{opensora , to overcome single GPU memory constraints, facilitating higher resolution and longer video generation.}}
elements.sent-2411-076: A major challenge with 3D VAE adoption is geometric control.
elements.sent-2411-077: As illustrated in \figref{fig:control , geometric control manages per-frame content spatially.}
elements.sent-2411-078: With 2D VAE, $T$ frames are encoded into $T$ latents.
elements.sent-2411-079: Using $T$ geometric descriptors $\{\mb{S _{t}\}, t\in\{1,...,T\}$, video geometric control degrades to spatial control on images~\cite{meng2022sdedit , as control signals and latents are temporally aligned.}}
elements.sent-2411-080: However, 3D VAE yields $T/f$ latents (where $f$ is the temporal compression ratio), misaligning control signals with latents and rendering previous control techniques~\cite{magicdrive,ma2024unleashing,wen2024panacea ineffective.}

elements.sent-2411-090: To expedite model convergence during training, we schedule the training data based on the duration of single training iterations.
elements.sent-2411-091: Specifically, we adopt a three-stage training approach: initially using low-resolution images for bootstrap training, transitioning to low-resolution short videos, and ultimately employing high-resolution long video training.
elements.sent-2411-092: This training strategy is grounded in two observations.
elements.sent-2411-093: First, in the controllable generation, we note that the model initially optimizes for higher content quality before learning controllability, a pattern also observed by \citet{gao2024vista .}
elements.sent-2411-094: Training the model from scratch requires numerous iterations for convergence.
elements.sent-2411-095: Our progressive transition approach enables the model to acquire controllability more rapidly.
elements.sent-2411-096: Second, during the stage transition, we find that the model adapts more quickly to high resolution~\cite{chen2024pixart compared to long-video controllability.}
elements.sent-2411-097: Therefore, we train the controllability from the first stage and focus on optimizing more iterations with (short) videos rather than images.

elements.sent-2411-098: As stated in \secref{sec:bootstrap , we employ a three-stage training process.}
elements.sent-2411-099: In the last two stages, we incorporate videos of varying resolutions and lengths for training.
elements.sent-2411-100: Specifically, in the second stage, we train with a mixture of videos up to 65 frames in length and a maximum resolution of 424$\times$800.
elements.sent-2411-101: In the third stage, we mix videos up to 241 frames (the maximum frame count of the dataset) and a resolution of up to 848$\times$1600 (the maximum resolution of dataset).
elements.sent-2411-102: Details of the mixing method are provided in the \appref{app:mix .}
elements.sent-2411-103: Compared to training with a single resolution and length, this mixed approach allows the model to rapidly comprehend information across the dimensions of resolution and frame count (\secref{sec:ablation holds more ablation study for this).}

elements.sent-2411-228: The rapid advancement of diffusion models has greatly improved video synthesis, especially in controllable video generation, which is vital for applications like autonomous driving.
elements.sent-2411-229: Although DiT with 3D VAE has become a standard framework for video generation, it introduces challenges in controllable driving video generation, especially for geometry control, rendering existing control methods ineffective.
elements.sent-2411-230: To address these issues, we propose \methodname, a novel approach that integrates the MVDiT block and spatial-temporal conditional encoding to enable multi-view video generation and precise geometric control.
elements.sent-2411-231: Additionally, we introduce an efficient method for obtaining contextual descriptions for videos to support diverse textual control, along with a progressive training strategy using mixed video data to enhance training efficiency and generalizability.
elements.sent-2411-232: Consequently, \methodname enables multi-view driving video synthesis with $3.3\times$ resolution and $4\times$ frame count (compared to current SOTA), rich contextual control, and geometric controls.
elements.sent-2411-233: Extensive experiments demonstrate \methodname's ability, unlocking broader applications in autonomous driving.. \urlabs.
elements.sent-2411-234: The rapid advancements in diffusion models and controllable generation has substantially broadened its range of applications~\cite{li2024trackdiffusion,wang2024detdiffusion .}
elements.sent-2411-235: Especially for autonomous driving, controllable generation of high-resolution and long videos has become a critical research focus~\cite{ma2024unleashing,yang2024drivearena .}
elements.sent-2411-236: High resolution facilitates the identification of fine details~\cite{liang2022bevfusion , while long videos provide richer interaction for evaluating and improving algorithms~\cite{yang2024drivearena .}}

elements.sent-2411-239: The introduction of DiT~\cite{peebles2023scalable,opensora and 3D VAE~\cite{opensora,yang2024cogvideox has substantially alleviated these challenges.}}
elements.sent-2411-240: DiT enables quality improvements in generation with more model parameters and extensive data.
elements.sent-2411-241: Consequently, many text-to-video methods leverage DiT with 3D VAE for high-quality generation~\cite{opensora,yang2024cogvideox .}
elements.sent-2411-242: However, autonomous driving requires multi-view generation with geometry control.
elements.sent-2411-243: How to effectively leverage the DiT with 3D VAE to achieve such controllable generation remains an open problem.
elements.sent-2411-244: One of the most critical issue from controllable generation in autonomous driving is geometry control, which involves the positioning of objects and the road structures.
elements.sent-2411-245: These geometric conditions are typically time-dependent, meaning they correspond to individual video frames in a one-to-one manner.
elements.sent-2411-246: With 2D VAEs~\cite{rombach2021highresolution,blattmann2023stable , this characteristic allows control methods designed for images~\cite{zhang2023adding to be seamlessly extended to video generation~\cite{magicdrive .}}}
elements.sent-2411-247: However, 3D VAE~\cite{opensora,yang2024cogvideox disrupts this alignment, as illustrated in \figref{fig:control .}}
elements.sent-2411-248: The spatial-temporal latent of 3D VAE requires that the control conditions also encapsulate corresponding spatial-temporal information.
elements.sent-2411-249: Therefore, it is necessary to redesign the geometry control module to accommodate the DiT with 3D VAE framework for driving video generation.

elements.sent-2411-250: In this paper, we present \methodname, a novel approach based on the DiT architecture, designed to tackle the challenges of high-resolution and long street-view video synthesis with precise control.
elements.sent-2411-251: Building upon DiT~\cite{peebles2023scalable,opensora integrated with a 3D VAE for geometric control, we introduce an MVDiT block for multi-view video generation, and a novel spatial-temporal conditional encoding for latents derived from CogVAE~\cite{yang2024cogvideox , enabling precise management of geometric controls.}}
elements.sent-2411-252: Additionally, we enhance contextual description supports by efficiently generating captions for each video.
elements.sent-2411-253: To train the model efficiently, we employ a progressive training strategy, transitioning from short to long videos, which helps the model capture intricate details and generalize to complex scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-254: Furthermore, we utilize videos of diverse resolutions and durations to enhance the model's generalization capability, enabling it to synthesize longer videos than those used in training.
elements.sent-2411-255: Our \methodname excels in generating highly realistic videos that align with text, road maps, 3D bounding boxes, and different camera perspectives, achieving significantly higher resolution and more frames than previous works (detailed in \tabref{tab:res-frame ).}
elements.sent-2411-256: Experiments and comparisons demonstrate the effectiveness of our training and control methods, significantly improving controllable street-view video synthesis.
elements.sent-2411-257: We design an efficient framework, \methodname, leveraging progressive training to achieve high-quality, high-resolution, and long video generation (up to $848\times1600\times6$, 241 frames), significantly superior to prior works.
elements.sent-2411-258: For geometric controls, novel spatial-temporal control for objects, road semantics, and camera trajectories is proposed while maintaining multi-frame, multi-view consistency.
elements.sent-2411-259: For text control, we enrich the contextual description on driving datasets to support diverse generation.

elements.sent-2411-290: However, our experiments reveal that this operation results in trailing issues (as "Box Reduce" in \figref{fig:abl_vis ), which we hypothesize are caused by the repeat operation.}

elements.subsection-2411-010: Enrich Text Control by Image Caption
elements.sent-2411-293: Although geometric descriptors offer precise control over objects and maps, textual descriptions in autonomous driving datasets (\eg, nuScenes) are often restricted to basic weather (sunny/rain) and time (day/night), lacking richer scene context such as road types (\eg, highway/urban) or background elements (\eg, buildings, trees).
elements.sent-2411-294: To enhance textual control, we regenerated video captions using a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM)~\cite{hurst2024gpt .}
elements.sent-2411-295: By prompting MLLM to focus on aspects beyond object categories, trajectories, and road structures, we ensure efficiency and consistency.
elements.sent-2411-296: Specifically, first, it eliminates the need for the MLLM to describe dynamics, thus only the middle frame of each video is used; second, it avoids potential conflicts between multiple control signals.
elements.subsection-2411-011: Progressive Training with Mixed Video Types
elements.sent-2411-297: To accelerate model convergence, we employ a three-stage training strategy: starting with low-resolution images, transitioning to high-resolution short videos, and finally using high-resolution long videos.
elements.sent-2411-298: This approach is based on two key insights.
elements.sent-2411-299: First, in controllable generation, models prioritize content quality before learning controllability, also observed by \citet{gao2024vista .}
elements.sent-2411-300: Our progressive training enables faster controllability acquisition.
elements.sent-2411-301: Second, models adapt more quickly to high resolution than to long videos, so we emphasize short-video training early on.
elements.sent-2411-302: In the third stage, we mix videos up to 241 frames (the maximum frame count of the dataset) at 224$\times$400 and a resolution of up to 848$\times$1600 (the maximum resolution of nuScenes~\cite{nuScenes ) at 33 frames.}
